svg.title: Banking data profiling agent architecture
svg.desc: Architecture diagram showing the profiling agent: banking raw data enters a Spark data reader, then a LangGraph agent orchestrates four tools (schema, stats, pattern, relation), feeds into LLM interpretation, outputs a Pydantic ProfileReport persisted to Delta Lake and passed to the modeling agent.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What synthetic banking tables should I generate with Faker for the profiling agent?')
g.text: accounts · transactions · customers · loans · collateral · risk ratings (Faker synthetic)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the Spark data reader handle Delta, Parquet and CSV sources in Docker?')
g.text: PySpark · delta-spark · reads Delta tables · Parquet · CSV · infers schema

svg.text: orchestrates tool calls · manages state · Pydantic v2 · LangChain · LiteLLM

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the schema tool detect — what exact outputs does it produce?')
sendPrompt('What statistical metrics does the stats tool compute on Spark DataFrames?')
sendPrompt('How does the pattern tool detect business key candidates and ID patterns in banking data?')
g.text: BK candidates · ID regex · date formats

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the relation tool infer foreign key hints and join candidates across banking tables?')
g.text: FK hints · join candidates · RI checks

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the LLM interpretation layer do with the four tool outputs — what prompt does it use?')
g.text: synthesises all tool outputs · suggests entity names · flags DQ issues · confidence scores

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the Pydantic ProfileReport schema look like — show me the model fields?')
g.text: tables · columns · BK suggestions · DQ flags · entity hints · confidence scores

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do I persist the ProfileReport as a Delta Lake table in the Docker setup?')
sendPrompt('How does the ProfileReport feed into the modeling agent as its input?')
